_note: Acceptance criteria for the live performance-acceptance harness (Context B; cabal executable perf-acceptance, task perf-acceptance). Version-controlled on purpose: moving the bar is an explicit, reviewed act. Two overheads are budgeted per package. `defaultBudgetMs`/`perPackageBudgetMs` bound the full-packument work-per-request (decode -> project -> rule sweep -> merge -> served-body assembly with the fused rewrite -> re-serialise) over a single live packument. `defaultSingleVersionBudgetMs`/`perPackageSingleVersionBudgetMs` bound the single-version selective decode the cold tarball gate consults to serve one package version (its latest) -- the per-package overhead a whole-document decode dominates and a selective decode does not, tracked on its own so a single-version improvement stays visible. A breach of either leg reds the (non-required) check to prompt a human decision -- code regression vs reality outgrew the budget -- and never blocks merge. These bounds are calibrated from the per-leg observed maxima across the seven daily CI runs of 2026-07-05 to 2026-07-11, with roughly 2.2x headroom to absorb shared-runner noise and real-world packument growth; the run summary's watch marks (a leg at 70%+ of its budget) give early warning before a red. Recalibrate the same way as the real runs accrue -- take the recent observed maxima from the run summaries and re-apply the headroom; that is what version-controlling them is for.
